description: This figure characterizes retained austenite (RA) evolution in Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si low-density steel under different thermomechanical processing conditions. Subplots (a)-(d) are EBSD phase maps for hot-rolled (HR), 750℃ annealed (AN750), 800℃ annealed (AN800), and 850℃ annealed (AN850) samples respectively: green denotes FCC RA, red denotes BCC matrix phases. RA fraction is 0 vol% for HR, 3.0 vol% (average size 0.59 μm) for AN750, 1.1 vol% (average size 0.72 μm) for AN800, 0.2 vol% (average size 0.83 μm) for AN850. Subplot (e) is a statistical plot summarizing RA content trend, which peaks at AN750 and decreases with higher annealing temperature, while average RA size increases with annealing temperature. Subplot (f) shows XRD patterns identifying α (BCC) and γ (FCC) phases, with γ peak intensity consistent with the RA content trend, confirming intercritical annealing at 750℃ yields the highest RA content for the studied steel.
